rationale.R1.1: A general opinion on the artifact centrally invites assessment of the thesis, gap framing, and contribution claim.
rationale.R1.2: The request implies evaluating whether the argumentative chain from motivation to method and claimed contribution holds together.
rationale.R1.3: Because the artifact reports quantitative results and uses them to support claims, evidence-claim alignment is centrally implicated.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact makes first-study and strongest-baseline claims, so novelty relative to prior work is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.7: The artifact relies on named prior work and literature positioning, making citation and scholarship assessment central.
rationale.RX.1: Any projected response should disclose uncertainty because the artifact is only an excerpt and no external verification is requested.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must guard against accepting or amplifying strong claims such as first-study or strongest-method without qualification.
rationale.RX.3: The response must remain within projection rather than executing the requested opinion or reviewing the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The response should avoid enabling misleading scholarly claims or unsafe downstream confidence claims.
rationale.RX.5: The basis for projected responsibilities should remain traceable to features explicitly present in the delegation and artifact.
